artifacts.content:
From: daniel.holloway84@gmail.com
To: rachel.holloway@outlook.com
Subject: Monday/Wednesday juggling act
Date: April 8, 2024

Hey Rach,

Typing this from the Deep River office before the after‑school crew rolls in. I wanted to get on the same page about the next few weeks, especially Mondays and Wednesdays, before our calendars get away from us again.

Mondays: I really want to keep that men’s group at New Hope Community Church on my schedule. You know the one I’ve been going to off and on since late 2022, the support group for guys talking about stress and burnout. The last couple of months with Mom’s arthritis flaring, Evan’s stuff, and work ramping up for the fall 5K, I’ve actually needed that space more. It’s the only time I’m not “Coach Daniel” or “Dad” or the one sorting out everyone’s problems. So as much as I hate being out another evening, I’d like us to treat that 7–8:30 p.m. block like an appointment I can’t skip.

Wednesdays: I’m still planning to drive over to Winston after work. Mom’s grocery runs are part of it, but I’ve also got that ongoing thing with the social worker at Novant Forsyth. Ever since her fall back in November 2023, they’ve been talking with me about in‑home help and even the possibility of assisted living down the line. Dad’s still digging in his heels, and he doesn’t want folks knowing we’re even discussing it, so I feel like I’ve got to keep showing up in person.

On top of that, Carla’s leaning on me more. After Anthony told her about the sports betting mess in January — that credit card bill he ran up on those apps — I promised I’d help them stick to the repayment plan we sketched out at their kitchen table. It’s not something I want Mom and Dad or even the kids hearing the full story about yet.

Can we look at the kids’ practice schedules tonight and make sure I’m not leaving you stranded on homework duty those nights? I can shift some afternoon stuff at Deep River, but evenings are pretty much off the table for me right now.

Love you,
D

From: daniel.holloway84@gmail.com
To: melissa.grant@highpointnc.gov
Subject: Deep River 5K volunteer coverage + course duties
Date: May 21, 2024

Hey Melissa,

I’ve been looking over the draft plan for the Deep River 5K & Family Fun Run and wanted to flag a couple of things while there’s still time to adjust.

First, on race day roles: I’m happy to take the lead on coordinating youth and community volunteers at the pavilion and along the family loop, but I probably shouldn’t run the course myself this year. The chronic knee pain that flares up after long Saturdays has gotten worse, and I finally started seeing a physical therapist — Dr. Karen Ellis at Cone Health Outpatient Rehab here in High Point — back in February 2024. She’s got me doing strengthening work and specifically told me not to push distance running for a while. I’ll still be mobile for setup, just not racing.

Second, about vendors and parent interactions. I really want this 5K to be clean and by‑the‑book. I know you talked with me in May about that parent complaint over age brackets — the one that made it up to the director because I wouldn’t bump their son into an older division. I heard you when you said I need to stay consistent but also document things better so there’s no question about fairness. I do not want another issue like that hanging over me, especially tied to a high‑profile event.

Connected to that, I’ve been thinking a lot about how we assign vendor spots. Back in March I made an informal side arrangement with Miguel from Triad Snack Shack to give him a prime finish‑line spot in exchange for a small discount on snack packs for Evan’s basketball team. At the time it felt harmless, but the more I sit with it, the more it bothers me that it didn’t go through the usual city process. For this 5K, I’d prefer we do everything through the standard rotation and written agreements so there’s no perception of favoritism.

If you’re okay with it, I’d like my official role to be: volunteer coordination, kids’ warm‑up area, and post‑race family activities, with someone else listed as course marshal/race lead.

Happy to talk this through when you have a minute.

Thanks,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Deep River 5K volunteer coverage + course duties

artifacts.content:
Thread: Daniel & "Coach Mike" (assistant coach)
Date: February 2, 2024

Mike: You good to run the Saturday clinic this week? I can take check‑in if you want to focus on drills.

Daniel: Actually, could we flip that? I need you to take the lead on the on‑court stuff this Saturday.

Mike: Yeah man, no problem. Everything okay?

Daniel: Mostly. Evan’s got a follow‑up with that child psychologist in Winston — Dr. Lauren Patel at Brenner Children’s Hospital — Saturday morning. It’s about that panic episode he had back in October during the middle school game in the Deep River gym. The one where he froze and couldn’t breathe when the stands filled up.

Daniel: We’ve been doing evaluations with her to see if it’s an anxiety thing tied to performance and some junk on social media. I really want to be there with him and Rachel.

Mike: Oh wow, I didn’t realize it was that serious. Definitely go. I’ll handle the kids.

Daniel: Thanks. Also, my knee’s been barking after long days out here. I finally caved and started PT with a Cone Health therapist in February, so me not sprinting around the court for three straight hours is probably a good idea anyway.

Mike: Getting old, Coach 😂

Daniel: For real. Between that, Mom’s appointments in Winston, and trying to keep up with all the Deep River stuff, I’m feeling it.

Mike: Anything you need me to emphasize with the clinic kids?

Daniel: Just keep it fun, stick to the equal‑playing‑time rule, and if any parent starts pushing about bumping their kid up an age group, shut that down. After that complaint last spring where the dad went over my head because I wouldn’t move his son up, Melissa gave me a pretty clear warning about staying tight on the rules.

Mike: Got it. I’ll be the bad guy if needed.

Daniel: Appreciate you, man.

metadata.title: Text thread with Mike about Saturday clinic

artifacts.content:
Title: Stuff I don’t want to drop (mid‑2024)
Date: June 10, 2024
Location: Kitchen table, after kids’ bedtime

Just a brain dump so I don’t keep spinning this in my head.

1. Mom and Dad
- Mom’s arthritis + that fall in November 2023 shook all of us more than we admit. I’m still driving to Winston most Wednesdays.
- Keep following up with the social worker at Novant Health Forsyth in Winston‑Salem. We talked in March 2024 about in‑home assistance and maybe assisted living if things get worse. Dad hates the idea and doesn’t want anyone at church knowing we’re even considering it.
- Need to find a way to talk to him about safety without making him feel like we’re taking his independence.

2. Carla & Anthony
- January 2024: Carla told me about Anthony’s sports betting mess — big credit card balance from those apps.
- We sat at their kitchen table and built a repayment plan. I promised her I’d keep checking in on Wednesdays. Asked her not to tell Mom/Dad or even Rachel all the details yet so it doesn’t blow up into a bigger family fight.

3. Money here at home
- Remember August 2023 when we were two months behind on the Piedmont Federal mortgage after Mom’s medical bills? I still feel that in my chest.
- I even seriously thought about taking paid weekend shifts at Triad Elite Sports Complex in Greensboro. It would’ve helped, but it’s too close to what I do for the city, and the contract really frowns on outside rec work that competes with Parks & Rec.
- Goal: build up a real emergency fund so I never feel that close to the edge again.

4. Me
- Keep going to the men’s group at New Hope on Monday nights. It’s the only place I say out loud how overwhelmed I feel juggling Deep River, the kids, and aging parents.
- Don’t let guilt push me into giving up that time.

Breathe. One thing at a time.

metadata.title: Stuff I don’t want to drop (mid‑2024)

artifacts.content:
Event: PT follow‑up – right knee
Date: Tuesday, March 19, 2024
Time: 8:15–9:00 a.m.
Location: Cone Health Outpatient Rehabilitation – High Point (3rd floor clinic)

Notes:
- Follow‑up with Dr. Karen Ellis about the chronic right knee pain that keeps flaring up after long Saturdays running youth games at Deep River.
- Ask if the swelling after last weekend’s soccer jamboree is normal or if I overdid it.
- Clarify what I *can* do at the Deep River 5K: walking the course vs. jogging, how much time on my feet is okay.
- Need a clear answer on whether I should skip actually running the race this year and just stick to coordinating volunteers.
- Mention that I’ve been trying to squeeze in early morning runs 2–3 days a week and that sometimes the pain hits later in the day when I’m demonstrating drills for the kids.
- Bring list of over‑the‑counter meds I’ve been taking and ask if there’s anything better that won’t make me loopy during work.
- If she thinks this could get worse long‑term, ask about braces/orthotics so I can keep coaching and demonstrating without looking like I’m falling apart in front of parents.

Reminders:
- Leave house by 7:30 a.m. from 2147 Oakview Ridge so I can drop Evan and Chloe at school first.
- Let Melissa know I’ll be in around 9:30 a.m. instead of 8:30.
- Don’t forget insurance card + Red Cross CPR/First Aid card (just in case they want to copy it for their records).

metadata.title: PT follow‑up – right knee
metadata.location: Cone Health Outpatient Rehabilitation – High Point

artifacts.content:
From: daniel.holloway84@gmail.com
To: carla.holloway@ymail.com
Subject: Quick check‑in (and Wednesday plan)
Date: January 29, 2024

Hey C,

Just wanted to check in after last week and make sure you’re holding up okay.

I’ve been thinking a lot about that night at your place earlier this month — sitting at your kitchen table in Winston in January, going through Anthony’s credit card statements and seeing how much of that balance was from those sports betting apps. I know that was a gut punch. I’m glad he owned up to it, but I also know you’re the one staring at the numbers at 2 a.m.

I meant what I said: I’m with you both on this. The repayment plan we sketched out is realistic if you stick to it, and I’m okay driving over on Wednesdays for a while to help you go over things line by line. We don’t have to tell Mom and Dad the whole story yet; they’d focus on the gambling part and not the fact that you two are actually trying to fix it.

Just remember we’re all juggling stuff. Between Mom’s arthritis and that fall she had back in November 2023, I’m already meeting with a social worker at Novant Forsyth about possible in‑home help or even assisted living if it comes to that. Dad would absolutely lose it if he knew how far those conversations have gone, so for now it’s just me, the social worker, and you in the loop.

Add in the money scare Rachel and I had last August when we fell two months behind on the Piedmont Federal mortgage after Mom’s medical bills from the fall, and I get the panic you’re feeling. I even thought about picking up paid weekend shifts at that Triad Elite Sports Complex in Greensboro, even though it brushes right up against my city job’s rules. That’s how close it felt.

Point is: you’re not the only one making hard choices. Text me what time works best this Wednesday and I’ll plan my Deep River schedule around it.

Love you,
D

Event: Men’s support group – New Hope
Date: Monday, May 6, 2024
Time: 7:00–8:30 p.m.
Location: New Hope Community Church, Classroom B

Description:
Weekly men’s support group I’ve been attending off and on since late 2022. Small circle (8–10 guys) talking honestly about stress, burnout, marriage/parenting stuff, and caring for aging parents.

Tonight’s focus (for me):
- Balancing Deep River workload (spring soccer + fall festival + 5K planning) with being present for Evan and Chloe.
- Processing Evan’s evaluation with Dr. Lauren Patel at Brenner Children’s Hospital in Winston‑Salem after his panic episode during that crowded middle school basketball game at the Deep River gym back in October 2023. How to support him without making him feel “broken.”
- Worries about Mom: arthritis getting worse since her November 2023 fall, and the quiet meetings I’ve been having with the social worker at Novant Forsyth about in‑home care/assisted living while Dad is still saying absolutely not.
- Money anxiety hangover from August 2023 when we got two months behind on the Piedmont Federal mortgage after Mom’s medical bills, plus Carla calling me in January about Anthony’s sports betting debt.

Reminders:
- Tell Rachel I’m leaving by 6:30 so she’s not surprised.
- Turn ringer off but keep phone in pocket in case of emergency with kids or Mom.
- Share honestly about how tired I am instead of just cracking jokes.
- No volunteering for extra evening commitments at church; I need to keep weeknights mostly clear.

metadata.title: Men’s support group – New Hope
metadata.location: New Hope Community Church, Classroom B

artifacts.content:
Hi Rachel,

I hope you're having a great day at the clinic. I wanted to touch base about our schedule for this week and some volunteer opportunities I'm thinking about.

First off, our regular family routine is still on. I'll be handling Evan and Chloe's school drop-offs on my way to Deep River, as usual. Remember, I have meetings and field checks on Tuesday and Thursday mornings, and I can cover any morning tasks on Friday if needed. Sunday's church and family time remain sacred, so no work on that day.

Also, I've been considering volunteering more at the Deep River Recreation Center, especially with the youth sports programs and community events like the festivals, 5K runs, and the park clean-up days. It's something I really enjoy, and my mornings are generally free for it. I've got a valid NC Class C driver's license (number H840-392-17-642, expiring in August 2027), so I can help with equipment pick-ups or any transportation needs. Plus, no legal hiccups in my past—never been convicted of a felony or misdemeanor, so nothing to worry about there.

On a different note, I've been reminiscing about our time at 1023 W Greenway Street in Winston-Salem. It's hard to believe we moved from there in 2019! Our current place at 2147 Oakview Ridge Drive, High Point, zip code 27265, is certainly more convenient for my work. And speaking of locations, my alma mater, R.J. Reynolds High School in Winston-Salem (Class of 2002), holds so many memories. Those years, from 1998 to 2002, were truly formative.

Regarding professional credentials, I renewed my First Aid/CPR/AED certification this past March, valid until 2025. It’s part of keeping our community events safe and well-managed. I've always felt proud of my B.S. in Recreation and Parks Management from the University of North Carolina at Greensboro. Those four years (2002-08 to 2006-05) really set the foundation for everything I do today.

Finally, in case of emergencies, you can always reach me at my cell, (336) 555-2198, or at our home number. For added peace of mind, I've listed my sister Carla’s number as an emergency contact: (336) 555-7742.

Let's catch up tonight over dinner. I’m thinking stir-fry, but let me know if you have other ideas!

Love,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Planning Our Week and Volunteering Update
